form_error_client_id_format: ClientID must contain only numbers, lowercase letters, and hyphens
form_error_subnet: The IP address should be in the subnet %cidr%
form_error_gateway_ip: Lease can't have the IP address of the gateway
out_of_range_error: The address must not be within the DHCP range %start%–%end%
greater_range_start_error: The end of the range must be greater than the start
subnet_error: Range addresses must be in the same subnet
form_error_url_format: Enter a valid URL or file path
dns_test_not_ok_toast: Upstream %key% passed syntax check but failed the test. Verify the upstream settings
dns_test_parsing_error_toast: Failed to extract upstream name from line %number% in the %section% section. Check the line format
dns_test_warning_toast: Upstream %key% did not respond to the DNS query. Verify the upstream settings
rate_limit_subnet_len_ipv4_error: The IPv4 subnet prefix length should be between 0 and 32
rate_limit_subnet_len_ipv6_error: The IPv6 subnet prefix length should be between 0 and 128
encryption_key_input: Copy/paste your PEM-encoded private key for your certificate here.
encryption_plain_dns_error: To disable plain DNS, enable at least one encrypted DNS protocol
encryption_unable_read_key: Unable to read private key file
encryption_key_cert_mismatch: Private key does not match certificate
form_error_port_range: Enter port number in the range of %min%-%max%
clients_desc: Rules for devices connected to AdGuard Home
name: Name
auto_clients_desc: Information about the IP addresses of devices that can use AdGuard Home
updates_checked: A new version of AdGuard Home is available
rewrite_hosts_applied: Rewritten by the hosts file rule
ignore_domains_desc_log: Requests that match these rules are not displayed in Query log
ignore_domains_desc_stats: Requests that match these rules are not included in the statistics
autofix_warning_text: If you click "Fix", AdGuard Home will configure your system to use AdGuard Home DNS server.
autofix_warning_list:
It will perform these tasks:

Deactivate system DNSStubListener

Set DNS server address to 127.0.0.1

Replace symbolic link target of /etc/resolv.conf with /run/systemd/resolve/resolv.conf

Stop DNSStubListener (reload systemd-resolved service)
autofix_warning_result: As a result all DNS requests from your system will be processed by AdGuard Home by default.
check_not_found: Not found in your filter lists
install_static_ok: Good news! The static IP address is already configured
install_static_error: AdGuard Home cannot configure it automatically for this network interface. Please look for an instruction on how to do this manually.
install_static_configure: AdGuard Home has detected that the dynamic IP address %ip% is used. Do you want it to be set as your static address?
confirm_static_ip: AdGuard Home will configure %ip% to be your static IP address. Do you want to proceed?
filter_category_general_desc: Lists that block tracking and advertising on most of the devices
filter_category_security_desc: Lists designed specifically to block malicious, phishing, and scam domains
filter_category_regional_desc: Lists that focus on regional ads and tracking servers
port_53_faq_link: Port 53 is often occupied by "DNSStubListener" or "systemd-resolved" services. Please read this instruction on how to resolve this.
settings_filter_requests_desc: You can use Blocklists, Allowlists, and User rules to set up filtering rules
settings_browsing_security_desc: Block access to domains found in the database of malicious and phishing domains
settings_parental_control_desc: Block websites with content inappropriate for children
settings_safe_search_desc: Block inappropriate content in search results
settings_anonymize_client_ip_desc: Replace part of the IP address with zeros
form_value_value_min_error: Enter a value of %min_value% or greater
settings_statistics_desc: Display request and client statistics on the dashboard
settings_confirm_decrease_log_rotation_i: This will delete all logs older than %value%
settings_confirm_decrease_stats_rotation: This will delete all stats older than %value%
settings_confirm_clear_statistics_desc: This will clear all statistics. Query log will still be available
settings_confirm_clear_query_log_desc: This will delete all requests from Query log. Statistics will still be available
settings_tooltip_domain_names: Domain names, wildcards, or filtering rules
encryption_encrypted_dns_desc: The AdGuard Home admin interface uses HTTPS. The DNS server supports DNS-over-HTTPS, DNS-over-TLS, and DNS-over-QUIC
encryption_plain_dns_desc: Plain DNS is enabled by default. Disabling it forces all devices to use encrypted DNS. To do so, specify at least one encrypted DNS protocol
encryption_server_tooltip_1: If set, AdGuard Home will detect ClientIDs, respond to DDR queries, and perform additional connection validations. If not set, these features are disabled
encryption_server_tooltip_2: Must match a DNS name in the certificate
encryption_https_tooltip: This port provides HTTPS access for the AdGuard Home admin interface. It also provides DNS-over-HTTPS at the '/dns-query' location
encryption_dot_tooltip: This port is used to run a DNS-over-TLS server
encryption_doq_tooltip: This port is used to run a DNS-over-QUIC server
encryption_force_redirect: Automatically redirect from HTTP to HTTPS
encryption_certificates_desc: To use encryption, you must provide a valid SSL certificate chain for your domain. You can get a free SSL certificate from letsencrypt.org or buy one from a trusted Certificate Authority
encryption_disable_plain_dns_desc: Devices that do not support encrypted DNS may not work properly
encryption_confirm_clear_desc: All encryption settings will be reset to their default values
reset_dns_confirm_text: All DNS protocol settings will be reset to their default values
path_to_file_placeholder: Path to file: local or Internet address
tls_cert_path_option: Enter the full path to the certificate file
tls_cert_path_label: Full path to the certificate file
tls_key_path_option: Enter the full path to the private key file
tls_key_path_label: Full path to the private key file
tls_cert_modal_description: To use encryption, you must provide a valid TLS certificate chain for your domain. You can get a free TLS certificate from letsencrypt.org or buy one from a trusted Certificate Authority
dns_allowed_clients_desc: Only accepts requests from clients on this list
dns_allowed_clients_desc_2: To add a client, enter its CIDR, IP address, or ClientID
dns_blocking_mode_custom_ip_desc: Responds with a manually set IP address
dns_blocking_mode_default_desc: Responds with 0.0.0.0 for A records and :: for AAAA records if blocked by an Adblock-style rule. Responds with the IP address specified in the rule if blocked by a hosts-style rule
dns_blocking_mode_desc: Sets the response type to blocked requests
dns_blocking_mode_null_ip_desc: Responds with 0.0.0.0 for A and :: for AAAA
dns_bootstrap_dns_desc: Sets the DNS servers used to resolve DoH/DoT upstream resolver hostnames
dns_bootstrap_dns_desc_2: Enter IP addresses only. Comments are not allowed
dns_bootstrap_dns_label: Bootstrap DNS servers, one per line
dns_clear_cache_desc: This will clear your device's DNS cache and prompt it to request new information from the DNS server
dns_confirm_clear_cache_desc: Are you sure you want to clear the DNS cache?
dns_disallowed_clients_desc: Drops requests from clients on this list. This setting is ignored when there are entries in Allowed clients
dns_disallowed_domains_desc: Drops DNS queries that match these domains, and they don’t appear in Query log
dns_disallowed_domains_desc_2: Enter exact domain names, wildcards, or URL filter rules, such as example.org, *.example.org, or ||example.org^
dns_dnssec_desc: Adds the DNSSEC flag in outbound DNS queries and checks the result. Requires a DNSSEC-enabled resolver
dns_edns_client_subnet_desc: Adds EDNS Client Subnet (ECS) data to upstream requests and records ECS values in Query log
dns_edns_desc: Add EDNS Client Subnet (ECS) data to upstream requests and record ECS values in Query log. You can use the default client subnet or specify a custom IP address for ECS
dns_fallback_dns_desc: Sets fallback DNS servers to use when upstream DNS servers are unavailable
dns_fallback_dns_desc_2: Use the same syntax as for the upstream DNS servers
dns_fallback_dns_label: Fallback DNS servers, one per line
dns_fallback_dns_placeholder: IP addresses, sdns://, quic://, https://, h3://, or tls://
dns_ipv6_resolution_desc: Enables resolution of DNS queries for IPv6 addresses (AAAA records) and adds IPv6 hints to HTTPS responses
dns_optimistic_caching_desc: Responds from the cache even if the entries have expired and tries to update them
dns_override_max_ttl_desc: Sets the maximum time DNS responses are cached. Higher TTL values are reduced to this value
dns_override_min_ttl_desc: Sets the minimum time DNS responses are cached. Lower TTL values are increased to this value
dns_private_reverse_page_desc: Resolves PTR, SOA, and NS requests for ARPA domains with private IP addresses through private upstream servers, DHCP, and /etc/hosts. When disabled, responds to all such requests with NXDOMAIN
dns_private_reverse_resolvers_desc: Resolves PTR, SOA, and NS requests for ARPA domains with private IP addresses through private upstream servers, DHCP, and /etc/hosts
dns_private_reverse_resolvers_disabled_d: When disabled, responds to all such requests with NXDOMAIN
dns_private_reverse_resolve_clients_desc: Resolves client IP addresses to their hostnames using PTR queries. Uses private DNS servers for local clients and upstream servers for clients with public IP addresses
dns_private_reverse_resolve_clients_titl: Reverse lookup of client IP addresses
dns_private_reverse_servers_desc: Sets the DNS servers for private PTR, SOA, and NS requests. These requests come from clients with private IP addresses and ask for ARPA domains containing subnets from private IP address ranges
dns_private_reverse_servers_desc_2: If this field is empty, your operating system's default DNS resolvers are used, except for AdGuard Home IP addresses. By default, AdGuard Home uses the following reverse DNS resolvers: %value_1% and %value_2%
dns_private_reverse_servers_label: Private reverse DNS servers, one per line
dns_private_reverse_servers_placeholder: IP addresses, sdns://, quic://, https://, h3://, or tls://
dns_rate_limit_allowlist_desc: Specifies IP addresses excluded from rate limiting
dns_rate_limit_desc: Sets the number of requests per second allowed per client
dns_rate_limit_desc_2: A value of 0 means no limit
dns_server_addresses_configured_in_file: Server addresses are configured in the file %path%
dns_server_addresses_desc: Sets DNS server addresses through which AdGuard Home will be accessible
dns_server_addresses_desc_2: View tips for configuring upstream DNS servers and our list of trusted DNS providers
dns_server_addresses_placeholder: IP addresses, sdns://, quic://, https://, h3://, or tls://
dns_subnet_prefix_desc: Sets the subnet prefix length for %value% addresses used for rate limiting
dns_upstream_mode_desc: Determines how to route your network’s DNS queries to multiple upstream servers
dns_upstream_timeout_desc: Sets how long to wait for a response from upstream servers
upstream_dns_placeholder: IP addresses, sdns://, quic://, https://, h3://, or tls://
upstream_dns_addresses_faq: Learn more about configuring upstream DNS servers. Here is a list of known DNS providers to choose from.
upstream_dns_load_balancing_desc:
Query one upstream server at a time

AdGuard Home uses a weighted random algorithm to select servers with the lowest number of failed lookups and the lowest average lookup time
upstream_dns_parallel_requests_desc: Use parallel queries to speed up resolving by querying all upstream servers simultaneously
upstream_dns_fastest_addr_desc: Wait for responses from all DNS servers, measure the TCP connection speed for each server, and return the IP address of the server with the fastest connection speed
upstream_dns_fastest_addr_warning: This mode can significantly slow down DNS queries, if one or more upstream servers are not responding. Make sure that your upstream servers are stable and your upstream timeout is low
upstream_example_upstream_doh3: %value%: encrypted DNS-over-HTTPS with forced HTTP/3 and no fallback to HTTP/2
upstream_fallback_title: Fallback DNS servers, one per line
upstream_fallback_title_faq: Fallback DNS servers to use when upstream DNS servers are unavailable. Use the same syntax as for the upstream DNS servers
ip_addresses_placeholder: Enter IP addresses, one per line
upstream_bootstrap_dns_title: Bootstrap DNS servers, one per line
upstream_bootstrap_dns_faq: IP addresses of DNS servers used to resolve the IP addresses of DoH/DoT upstream resolvers. Comments are not allowed
upstream_ptr: Private reverse DNS servers, one per line
upstream_ptr_faq_1: AdGuard Home uses these DNS servers for private PTR, SOA, and NS requests. Private requests come from clients with a private IP address and ask for an ARPA domain that contains a subnet from a private IP address range, such as %value%
upstream_ptr_faq_2: If this field is empty, the default DNS resolvers of your OS will be used, except for the AdGuard Home IP addresses
upstream_ptr_faq_3: By default, AdGuard Home uses the following reverse DNS resolvers: %value_1% and %value_2%
upstream_private_ptr_resolvers_desc: Resolve PTR, SOA, and NS requests for ARPA domains containing private IP addresses through private upstream servers, DHCP, /etc/hosts, and so on. If disabled, AdGuard Home will respond to all such requests with NXDOMAIN
upstream_enable_reverse_lookup_title: Enable reverse lookup of client IP addresses
upstream_enable_reverse_lookup_desc: Reverse-resolve client IP addresses to their hostnames by sending PTR queries to appropriate resolvers: private DNS servers for local clients, or upstream servers for clients with public IP addresses
upstream_timeout_faq: Time to wait for a response from the upstream server
upstream_timeout_placeholder: Enter the upstream server timeout duration
server_config_rate_limit_faq: The number of requests per second allowed per client. A value of 0 means no limit
server_config_subnet_len_ipv4: Subnet prefix length for IPv4 addresses
server_config_subnet_len_ipv4_faq: Subnet prefix length for IPv4 addresses used for rate limiting. The default is 24
server_config_subnet_len_ipv6: Subnet prefix length for IPv6 addresses
server_config_subnet_len_ipv6_faq: Subnet prefix length for IPv6 addresses used for rate limiting. The default is 56
server_config_rate_limit_whitelist_faq: IP addresses excluded from rate limiting
server_config_edns_cs_desc: Add the EDNS Client Subnet (ECS) option to upstream requests and log the values sent by the clients in the query log
server_config_edns_use_custom_ip: Use custom IP address for EDNS
server_config_edns_use_custom_ip_desc: Replace the client IP address with the custom IP address below
server_config_dnssec_enable_desc: Set the DNSSEC flag in outbound DNS queries and check the result. Requires a DNSSEC-enabled resolver
server_config_disable_ipv6_desc: Drop all DNS queries for IPv6 addresses (type AAAA) and remove IPv6 hints from HTTPS responses
server_config_blocking_mode_default_desc: Default: Respond with 0.0.0.0 for A and :: for AAAA if blocked by Adblock-style rule. Respond with the IP address specified in the rule if blocked by /etc/hosts-style rule
server_config_blocking_mode_null_ip_desc: Null IP address: Respond with 0.0.0.0 for A and :: for AAAA
server_config_blocking_mode_custom_ip_de: Custom IP address: Respond with a manually set IP address
server_config_blocking_mode_ipv4_faq: IP address to return for a blocked A request
server_config_blocking_mode_ipv6_faq: IP address to return for a blocked AAAA request
server_config_blocking_mode_ttl_faq: Specifies how long clients should cache a filtered response
cache_config_size_faq: DNS cache size. To disable caching, leave empty
cache_config_min_ttl_faq: The minimum time-to-live (TTL) value for entries in the DNS cache
cache_config_max_ttl_faq: The maximum time-to-live (TTL) value for entries in the DNS cache
cache_config_optimistic_desc: AdGuard Home will respond from the cache even if the entries have expired and will try to update them
cache_config_size_validation: The cache size must be greater than zero when enabled
cache_config_ttl_validation: Minimum cache TTL override must be less than or equal to the maximum
cache_confirm_clear_desc: This will remove all cached DNS responses. Subsequent DNS queries may be slower until the cache is rebuilt
access_settings_allowed_faq: A list of CIDRs, IP addresses, or ClientIDs. If this field has entries, AdGuard Home will accept requests only from these clients
access_settings_disallowed_faq: A list of CIDRs, IP addresses, or ClientIDs. If this field has entries, AdGuard Home will drop requests from these clients. This field is ignored if there are entries in Allowed clients
access_settings_blocked_faq_1: AdGuard Home will drop DNS queries matching these domains. Such queries don't appear in the query log. You can enter exact domain names, wildcards, or URL filter rules, such as example.org, *.example.org, or ||example.org^
access_settings_blocked_faq_2: Not to be confused with filters
blocklists_desc: Domains from these lists are blocked
blocklists_add_desc: You can select blocklists from the list or add them manually
blocklists_empty: No blocklists yet — <button>add your first one</button>
allowlists_desc: Domains on these allowlists are allowed even if they are on a blocklist
update_filters_desc: Sets the auto-update interval for filters. Applies to both blocklists and allowlists added via external links
allowlist_empty: No allowlists yet — <button>add your first one</button>
dns_rewrite_same: You can’t rewrite to the same domain or wildcard
dns_rewrites_desc: Lists your custom DNS responses for specific domain names
rewrites_empty: No rewrites yet — <button>add your first one</button>
rewrite_domain_input_placeholder: Enter the domain name or wildcard
rewrites_answer_input_placeholder: Enter IP address, CNAME record, A, or AAAA
rewrites_remove_desc: The DNS rewrite for %value% will be removed
rewrites_tooltip_examples_item1: Rewrite DNS responses for this domain name only
rewrites_tooltip_examples_item2: Rewrite DNS responses for all subdomains of example.org
rewrites_tooltip_instructions_item1: To resolve the domain name to an IP address, enter an IPv4 address for A records and an IPv6 address for AAAA records
rewrites_tooltip_instructions_item2: To redirect the domain name, enter the CNAME record of the target domain
rewrites_tooltip_instructions_item3: To keep only A records, enter
rewrites_tooltip_instructions_item4: To keep only AАAA records, enter
all_rewrites_disabled: All custom DNS responses will be disabled
all_rewrites_enabled: All custom DNS responses will be enabled
setup_guide_desc: Configure your devices to use AdGuard Home
home_dns_addresses_desc: The AdGuard Home DNS server listens on the following interfaces/ports:
setup_devices_macos_list_2: Select the required connection and click Advanced.
setup_devices_macos_list_3: Select the DNS tab and enter the addresses of your AdGuard Home server.
setup_devices_android_list_1: Open your device's Settings app and select Wi-Fi.
setup_devices_android_list_2: Long press your current network, then select Modify network. It is not possible to set custom DNS for mobile Internet connections.
setup_devices_android_list_3: On some devices, you may need to select the Advanced checkbox to see more settings. To customize your Android DNS settings, you need to change IP settings from DHCP to Static.
setup_devices_android_list_4: Change the DNS 1 and DNS 2 values to the addresses of your AdGuard Home server.
setup_devices_ios_list_1: Open your device's Settings app and select Wi-Fi. It is not possible to set custom DNS for mobile Internet connections.
setup_devices_ios_list_2: Tap the name of the currently active network.
setup_devices_ios_list_3: Go to DNS → Configure DNS → Manual and add the addresses of your AdGuard Home server.
setup_devices_router_desc_1: Set up AdGuard Home on your home network router and protect all devices connected to it. No need to manually configure each device.
setup_devices_router_list_1: You can usually access them from your browser via a URL, such as http://192.168.0.1 or http://192.168.1.1. You may be asked to enter a password. You can often reset the password by pressing a button on the router itself, but be aware that you could lose your entire router configuration. If your router is to be set up through an app, install the app on your phone or PC and use it to access the router's settings.
setup_devices_router_list_2_title: In the DHCP/DNS settings, enter the addresses of your AdGuard Home server.
setup_devices_router_list_2: To find the settings, look for DNS next to a field that allows two or three sets of numbers, each divided into four groups of one to three digits.
setup_devices_router_desc_2: Some routers do not allow you to set up a custom DNS server. In this case, you can try to set up AdGuard Home as a DHCP server. If this does not work, please refer to your router manual for instructions on how to customize DNS servers for your specific router.
setup_devices_windows_list_1: Open Control Panel from the Start menu or Windows Search.
setup_devices_windows_list_2: Click Network and Internet → Network and Sharing Center → Change adapter settings.
setup_devices_windows_list_3: Right-click on your active connection and select Properties.
setup_devices_windows_list_4: In the list, select Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4) for IPv4 or Internet Protocol Version 6 (TCP/IPv6) for IPv6.
setup_devices_windows_list_5: Click Properties → Use the following DNS server addresses.
setup_devices_windows_list_6: Enter the addresses of your AdGuard Home server.
setup_devices_dns_list_devices: Here’s a list of software you can use.
setup_devices_dns_android_list_1: Android 9 natively supports DNS-over-TLS. To configure it, go to Settings → Network & Internet → Advanced → Private DNS and enter your domain name.
setup_devices_dns_ios_list_2: DNSCloak supports DNS-over-HTTPS, but to configure it to use your own server, you'll need to generate a DNS Stamp for it.
setup_devices_dns_other_list_1: AdGuard Home itself can be a secure DNS client on any platform.
setup_devices_dns_other_list_2: dnsproxy supports all known secure DNS protocols.
setup_devices_dns_other_list_5: For more implementations, see DNSCrypt and DNS privacy.
setup_devices_dns_macos_desc: On an iOS 14 or macOS Big Sur device, you can download a special mobileconfig file that adds DNS-over-HTTPS or DNS-over-TLS servers to the DNS settings.
client_id_faq: Clients can be identified by their ClientID. To learn more, visit the AdGuard Home GitHub
pause_for_seconds: | Pause for %count% second | Pause for %count% seconds
pause_for_minutes: | Pause for %count% minute | Pause for %count% minutes
no_stats_yet: No stats yet — keep using AdGuard Home to view them
confirm_client_block_desc: AdGuard Home will be dropping all DNS requests from the client %ip%
confirm_client_unblock_desc: DNS requests from the client %ip% will be allowed
dns_queries_tooltip: The number of DNS queries processed for the last 24 hours
ads_blocked_tooltip: The number of DNS queries blocked by adblock filters and hosts blocklist
threats_blocked_tooltip: The number of DNS queries blocked by the AdGuard browsing security module
adult_websites_blocked_tooltip: The number of adult websites blocked
safe_search_used_tooltip: The number of DNS queries to search engines for which Safe Search was enforced
average_time_processing_tooltip: Average time in milliseconds onprocessing a DNS query
setup_dns_notice_new: In order to use DNS-over-HTTPS or DNS-over-TLS, you need to configure Encryption in AdGuard Home settings.
setup_guide_greeting_desc: This wizard will help you configure AdGuard Home:
setup_guide_greeting_list_1: Create credentials to access the admin panel
setup_guide_greeting_list_2: Set the Web UI address for AdGuard Home
setup_guide_greeting_list_3: Set the AdGuard Home DNS server address
setup_guide_greeting_list_4: Get instructions for your individual devices
setup_guide_greeting_list_5: Get access to the AdGuard Home dashboard
setup_guide_auth_subtitle: This is required to protect the AdGuard Home admin panel from unauthorized access
setup_guide_auth_privacy: I accept the Privacy policy and Terms and conditions of AdGuard websites
password_requirements_special: English letters, numbers, and special characters
setup_complete: Setup is complete and AdGuard Home is ready to use
setup_ui_title: Set the Web UI address for AdGuard Home
setup_ui_desc: Use these addresses to access the Web user interface of AdGuard Home. If necessary, change the port
setup_dns_title: Set the AdGuard Home DNS server address
setup_dns_desc: Configure your router or other devices to use the following DNS server addresses:
setup_dns_quote_title: Assign a static IP address to the AdGuard Home DNS server on your local network
setup_dns_quote_desc: To function properly, the AdGuard Home DNS server requires a static IP address on your local network. Otherwise, your router may eventually assign it a different IP address and disrupt the DNS protection
setup_guide_title: Set up your devices to use AdGuard Home
port_in_use: This port is already in use
forgot_password_page_desc: AdGuard Home stores passwords as BCrypt-encrypted hashes
forgot_password_list_item_1: Create a new password using the htpasswd tool or any online BCrypt generator.
forgot_password_list_item_3: Open AdGuardHome.yaml, find the password field, and replace it with the new value.
forgot_password_list_item_5: Log in to the web interface with your new password.
period_notify: To change the displayed periods, edit Statistics retention in General settings
password_login_error: Your username and password do not match
dhcp_enable: Use AdGuard's own built-in DHCP server
unavailable_dhcp_desc: AdGuard Home cannot run a DHCP server on your OS
dhcp_warning: To enable this DHCP server, deactivate all other DHCP servers on your network. Otherwise, devices may lose Internet access
dhcp_error: Failed to check the DHCP server. Wait a bit before retrying
dhcp_static_ip_error: The DHCP server requires a static IP address. We could not confirm that this network interface has one
dhcp_dynamic_ip_found: Your system uses dynamic IP address configuration for interface %interface_name%. In order to use DHCP server, a static IP address must be set. Your current IP address is %ip%. AdGuard Home will automatically set this IP address as static if you press the "Enable DHCP server" button.
dhcp_reset: This will reset the DHCP configuration. All changes will be lost
dhcp_mac_address_already_added: This MAC address is already added
form_error_ip_already_added: This IP address is already added
form_error_mac_already_added: This MAC address is already added
form_error_hostname_format: Use numbers, lowercase English letters, or hyphens
make_static_desc: This action converts a dynamic address to a static one
query_log_strict_search: Use double quotes for strict search
disallow_client_confirm_text: This will drop all future DNS queries from client %ip% and remove it from the list of allowed clients
disallow_client_confirm_allowlist_note: This action also will exclude the rule %ip% from the list of allowed clients.
query_log_nothing_available_rotation: Query log is disabled in General settings
blocked_services_desc: Select popular websites and services that should be blocked
inactivity_schedule_desc: Set inactivity periods for blocking services
inactivity_schedule_delete_desc: Service blocking will be active all day on %value%
inactivity_schedule_replace_desc: This schedule will replace any existing schedules for the same day of the week. Each day can have only one inactivity period
user_rules_desc: Enter one rule per line. Use Adblock-style or hosts file syntax
user_rules_example_block: ||%domain% Block access to %domain% and all its subdomains
user_rules_example_unblock: @@||%domain% Unblock access to %domain% and all its subdomains
user_rules_example_response: 127.0.0.1 %domain% Respond with 127.0.0.1 for %domain% only, not for its subdomains
user_rules_example_comment: ! This is a comment To comment, start the line with ! or #
user_rules_example_comment_2: # This is also a comment To comment, start the line with ! or #
user_rules_example_regex: /REGEX/ Block access to domains that match the specified regular expression
user_rules_learn_more: Learn more about creating your own hosts lists.
user_rules_check_title: Check domain filtering globally or for a client
user_rules_check_client_label: Client identifier (name, ClientID, or IP address)
user_rules_rule_added_to_custom_filterin: Rule added to custom filtering rules
user_rules_rule_removed: Rule removed from custom filtering rules
clients_remove_desc: %value% will be removed from Persistent clients along with its settings
clients_identifiers_desc: You can use the client’s IP address, CIDR, MAC address, or ClientID (for DoT, DoH, and DoQ). Learn more
clients_identifier_format_error: Use numbers, lowercase English letters, or hyphens
clients_tags_desc: You can add tags for this client and include them in filtering rules. Learn more
clients_use_global_settings_desc: Inherit protection settings from your AdGuard Home account
clients_protection_desc: Enable or disable protection features, logging, and statistics
clients_upstreams_desc: Upstream DNS servers, one per line
clients_dont_collect_stats: Don't collect stats for this client
tls_certificate_expiring: Your TLS certificate is about to expire
update_failed: Auto-update failed. Please follow these steps to update manually
